architecture.summary: VoxPoser uses GPT-4 to generate Python code that orchestrates vision-language models (OWL-ViT, Segment Anything, XMEM) to compose 3D voxel value maps grounded in robot observation space. These value maps encode affordances, constraints, rotation, velocity, and gripper actions. The maps serve as objective functions for a model-predictive-control motion planner (zeroth-order optimization via random shooting) that synthesizes 6-DoF end-effector trajectories executed with replanning at 5 Hz. The system optionally learns environment dynamics models from online interactions to handle contact-rich tasks.
trained_components: dynamics model (MLP, learned from online interactions for contact-rich tasks)

biblio.title: VoxPoser: Composable 3D Value Maps for Robotic Manipulation with Language Models

claims.dataset: real-world domain (5 tasks: Move & Avoid, Set Up Table, Close Drawer, Open Bottle, Sweep Trash)
claims.statement: VoxPoser achieves 88.0% success rate on real-world everyday manipulation tasks (static environment), outperforming LLM+Primitives baseline at 24.0%

claims.dataset: simulated domain (13 randomizable tasks, 2766 unique instructions)
claims.statement: VoxPoser achieves 77.5% success rate on spatial composition tasks with seen instructions and seen attributes in simulation, compared to 53.8% for U-Net motion planning baseline and 43.8% for LLM+Primitives

claims.dataset: simulated domain (Door task with MLP dynamics model)
claims.statement: VoxPoser with zero-shot trajectory prior achieves 88.3% success on door-opening tasks with online dynamics learning in 142.3 seconds, compared to no-prior baseline that times out after 12 hours
VoxPoser maintains high success rates across unseen instructions and unseen attributes: 60.0% on object interaction (UI/UA), 58.8% on spatial composition (SI/UA), 65.0% on object interaction (UI/UA)
VoxPoser exhibits lowest specification error compared to learned cost specification (U-Net) and LLM+Primitives due to its generalization and flexibility in spatial composition
VoxPoser is robust to external disturbances such as moving targets, obstacles, and perturbations (e.g., pulling drawer open after robot closes it) due to fast replanning at 5 Hz

VoxPoser relies on external perception modules, which is limiting in tasks requiring holistic visual reasoning or understanding of fine-grained object geometries; most real-world errors are attributed to perception module sensitivity
VoxPoser requires manual prompt engineering for LLMs and currently uses end-effector-only trajectory planning rather than whole-arm planning
